alert.description: P99 request latency critically exceeds SLO threshold

logs.message: Deployment: checkout-service v2.3.1 rolled out successfully (0 errors during rollout)
Redis cache miss rate elevated: 34% (threshold 30%) — cache warming in progress
DB connection pool: 8/10 connections in use
JVM GC pause: 420ms stop-the-world collection (heap 78% utilised) — within acceptable range
Health check /actuator/health returned 503 from upstream load balancer — retrying (attempt 1/3)
Health check recovered after retry — upstream responding normally
DB connection pool: 10/10 connections in use, queuing requests
Stripe webhook delivery delayed 1200ms (SLA=500ms) — possible downstream congestion
DB connection pool exhausted — waiting for available connection (timeout=30s)
Query timeout: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id=12345 (full table scan, duration=30s)
DB connection pool exhausted — waiting for available connection (timeout=30s)
Datadog agent: metric submission backlog 850 points — host under load
SQLSTATE[HY000]: General error: too many connections
checkout-service: request queue depth=450, dropping new requests

root_cause: Missing index on orders.user_id causing full table scan. At high load each scan holds a DB connection for 30s+, exhausting the pool of 10 connections.
